 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.4","title":"Implement dsr fallback command - trigger local build pipeline","description":"# Implement `dsr fallback` — Orchestrated Build + Release

## Purpose
End-to-end fallback pipeline when GH Actions is throttled: build, verify, release, and report.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr fallback <tool>
 dsr fallback <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr fallback <tool> --build-only
 dsr fallback <tool> --skip-checks
 dsr fallback <tool> --dry-run
 dsr fallback <tool> --resume
```

## Flow (must be deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (build from tag)
2. Run pre-release checks (unless --skip-checks)
3. Acquire lock + create run_id
4. Build all targets (act + native hosts)
5. Generate manifest + checksums + signatures
6. Verify artifacts
7. Create release and upload assets
8. Emit JSON summary + status report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end without manual intervention
- [ ] Uses manifest for release validation
- [ ] Supports resume after partial failure
- [ ] Clear logs + JSON output (schema-compliant)
- [ ] --dry-run outputs a deterministic plan without side  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.5","title":"Create default repos.d registry with all 16 tools configured","description":"# Create Default repos.d Registry with All 16 Tools Configured

## Purpose
Ship a complete, ready-to-use repository registry so `dsr` can build/release/install the full Dicklesworthstone toolchain without manual setup.

## Why this matters
Most commands (`check`, `build`, `release`, installers) rely on accurate repo metadata. Missing or inconsistent entries cause brittle behavior and user confusion.

## Location
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/` (created by `dsr config init`)
- One file per tool: `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Each file contains `schema_version` + tool fields

## Required Fields (per tool)
- `repo` (owner/name)
- `local_path` (absolute path, usually under `/data/projects`, but allow overrides)
- `language` (rust|go|bun|node)
- `binary_name`
- `build_cmd` (reproducible, non-interactive)
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (lint/test/typecheck commands)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`
- `enabled` (bool) to allow opt-out

## Guardrails
- Never create new directories under `/data/projects`
- Missing `local_path` → warn, do not mutate
- Use `yq` for safe writes when available; read-only fallback otherwise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools configured with full metadata
- [ ] Paths validated (existence check + absolute)
- [ ] Workflows + job mappings verified
- [ ] Artifact naming matches installer expectations
- [ ] Registry lives in `repos.d/` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:13.167130536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.294176214Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.293673988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:33.023057790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.6","title":"Implement notification system for build status","description":"# Notification System for Build Status

## Purpose
Notify users when builds complete/fail or need attention, without leaking secrets or blocking automation.

## Channels
- Terminal (default, stderr)
- Agent Mail (MCP) when available
- Slack webhook (optional)
- Email (optional, future)

## Design Requirements
- **Non-interactive safe**: no prompts when `--non-interactive`
- **Redaction**: tokens/secrets never appear in notifications or logs
- **Deduplication** by `run_id` (one notification per state transition)
- **Structured log events** so notifications are traceable

## Agent Mail Integration (Best Practice)
- Use MCP `send_message` when available
- Fall back gracefully if server not running
- Provide a short summary + path to logs/manifests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terminal notifications on macOS/Linux
- [ ] Agent Mail integration works when available
- [ ] Slack/email optional and safe
- [ ] Notifications are non-blocking and logged
- [ ] Duplicate alerts suppressed per run_id","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:24:21.269569798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:00.097906097Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.732813620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:42.109646409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.1.7","title":"Implement auto-tag from version files","description":"# Auto-Tag from Version Files

## Purpose
Automatically create git tags when version files (Cargo.toml, package.json, VERSION, etc.) are updated. This keeps releases consistent across tools and avoids manual tagging errors.

## Version Sources (configurable)
| Language | File | Pattern |
|----------|------|---------|
| Rust | Cargo.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Go | VERSION or version.go | Version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Bun/Node | package.json | "version": "X.Y.Z" |
| Python | pyproject.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |

## Implementation Notes
- Use repo registry (`repos.d/<tool>.yaml`) to resolve `local_path`
- **No global cd** — use `git -C <repo>`
- Dirty tree detection via exit codes (`git diff-index --quiet HEAD --`)
- Tag existence via `git show-ref --tags -d "refs/tags/vX.Y.Z"`
- Support `--dry-run` and `--non-interactive`

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr version detect <tool>   # Show current version
dsr version tag <tool>      # Create tag if needed
dsr version tag --all       # Tag all repos with new versions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect version from all supported language files
- [ ] Skip if tag already exists
- [ ] Create annotated tags
- [ ] Push tags to remote
- [ ] All git operations use `git -C` (no global cd)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:57.429934130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:08:12.508139810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.309988979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:54.300293741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands. Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN

- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:51:40.191796583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.4","title":"Secrets & credential loading (gh/PAT/slack)","description":"# Secrets & Credential Loading (gh/PAT/Slack)

## Purpose
Normalize how `dsr` discovers and validates credentials without leaking secrets.

## Sources (precedence)
1. Environment variables (DSR_GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, DSR_SLACK_WEBHOOK)
2. Config file entries (optional, discouraged unless encrypted)
3. `gh auth token` fallback

## Requirements
- Never echo secrets to logs
- Redact tokens in any JSON output
- Validate scopes (contents:write for releases; workflow for dispatch)
- Support non-interactive errors with actionable messages
- Never write secrets to disk unless explicitly configured

## Redaction Rules
- Any value matching token patterns must be masked in logs
- `config show --json` must redact secrets ("***")

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tokens resolved deterministically with clear precedence
- [ ] Missing/insufficient scopes detected early (doctor + runtime)
- [ ] Secrets never written to disk in plain logs
- [ ] Redaction applied consistently in structured logs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:08.437866552Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:26:08.437848668Z","close_reason":"Implemented secrets module with token resolution, redaction, scope validation, and doctor integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.109028754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:03.408338697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:43.753410065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:27:45.903498843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7.5","title":"Runtime guardrails: Bash version + path normalization","description":"# Runtime Guardrails: Bash Version + Path Normalization

## Purpose
Harden dsr runtime behavior to prevent subtle failures (Bash version mismatch, relative path writes, unsafe deletions) and mirror the reliability of `ru`.

## Requirements
- **Bash 4+ gate** with actionable guidance (macOS: brew install bash)
- **Absolute path enforcement** for `--config`, `--state-dir`, `--cache-dir` (accept `~`)
- **Safe temp dirs**: always use `/tmp` or `mktemp -d` (never create in `/data/projects`)
- **Safe delete helper**: allowlist deletions to `DSR_STATE_DIR` + `DSR_CACHE_DIR`
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** disables ANSI across all commands
- **Non-interactive** errors: never prompt; return actionable error messages

## Implementation Notes
- Add `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default` helper (ru parity)
- Add `require_bash_4` helper (exit 3 on failure)
- Add `safe_path_under <root> <path>` and `safe_rm <path>`

 {"id":"bd-1nf","title":"Test infrastructure: structured logging for tests","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Structured Logging for Tests

## Purpose
Provide consistent, detailed logging infrastructure for all test files to improve debugging and CI visibility.

## Features

### 1. Log Levels
- DEBUG: Detailed step-by-step info
- INFO: Test progress and key events
- PASS: Successful assertions
- FAIL: Failed assertions with context
- SKIP: Skipped tests with reason

### 2. Output Formatting
```
[2026-01-30 12:34:56] [INFO] test_config.sh: Starting 12 tests
[2026-01-30 12:34:56] [DEBUG] test_init: Creating temp dir /tmp/dsr-test-xyz
[2026-01-30 12:34:57] [PASS] test_init: config_init creates directories
[2026-01-30 12:34:58] [FAIL] test_load: Expected 'info' got 'debug'
  -> File: test_config.sh:45
  -> Expected: log_level = info
  -> Actual: log_level = debug
```

### 3. File Output
- Per-test log files: `logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/test_*.log`
- Aggregated summary: `logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/summary.json`

### 4. Failure Capture
- Full stdout/stderr on failure
- Last N lines of relevant log
- Environment snapshot (key vars)

## API
```bash
source test_logging.sh
test_log_init "test_config.sh"
test_log_debug "Creating temp directory"
test_log_pass "config_init creates directories"
test_log_fail "Expected 'info' got 'debug'" "test_config.sh:45"
test_log_summary  # Print final results
```

## Integration
- Drop-in for existing test_*.sh files
- No external dependencies
- Works standalone and with test runner

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Consistent format across all tests
- [ ] Timestamps in ISO8601
- [ ] Failure context includes file:line
- [ ] Summary shows pass/fail/skip counts
- [ ] Works with run-all-tests.sh","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.915636177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:49:00.949798624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nf","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.915636177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Module Overview
config.sh provides XDG-compliant configuration management:
- `config_init` - Create default config files
- `config_load` - Load config into DSR_CONFIG array
- `config_get` - Retrieve config values with defaults
- `config_set` - Update config values
- `config_validate` - Schema validation
- `config_show` - Display configuration

### 1. Initialization Tests
- `config_init` creates all required directories
- Creates default config.yaml with valid schema
- Creates hosts.yaml with default hosts
- Creates repos.yaml with empty registry
- Creates secrets/ directory with proper permissions (700)
- Does NOT overwrite existing config (unless --force)
- --force flag recreates config from scratch

### 2. Loading Tests
- `config_load` populates DSR_CONFIG associative array
- Missing config file triggers error with helpful message
- Malformed YAML produces clear error
- Default values applied for missing keys

### 3. Get/Set Tests
- `config_get` returns correct values
- `config_get` with default returns default when key missing
- `config_set` updates in-memory value
- `config_set --persist` writes to disk
- Nested key access (e.g., `signing.enabled`)

### 4. Validation Tests
- Valid config passes validation
- Invalid schema_version fails
- Missing required fields fail
- Type mismatches detected (string vs int)

### 5. XDG Compliance Tests
- Respects XDG_CONFIG_HOME override
- Respects XDG_CACHE_HOME override
- Respects XDG_STATE_HOME override
- Falls back to ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/state

## Test Approach
- Use TEMP_DIR for isolated testing
- Set DSR_CONFIG_DIR to temp directory
- Clean up after each test
- NO mocking - test real file operations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:36:05.370272393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:40.632391316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lm","title":"Test infrastructure: run-all-tests.sh runner script","description":"# Test Infrastructure: run-all-tests.sh Runner Script

## Purpose
Create a unified test runner that executes all tests with detailed logging, summary reporting, and CI-friendly output.

## Features

### 1. Test Discovery
- Auto-discover all test_*.sh files in scripts/tests/
- Support for --filter to run specific tests
- Support for --exclude to skip tests
- List mode: show discovered tests without running

### 2. Execution
- Run each test in isolated subshell
- Capture stdout and stderr separately
- Capture exit codes
- Enforce timeout per test (configurable, default 60s)
- Support for parallel execution (--parallel N)

### 3. Logging
- Per-test log files in logs/tests/YYYY-MM-DD/
- Structured JSON summary at end
- Human-readable progress output to stderr
- Color-coded results (pass/fail/skip)
- Verbose mode shows full test output

### 4. Reporting
- Total tests: run, passed, failed, skipped
- Duration per test and total
- List of failed tests with error snippets
- Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail

### 5. CI Integration
- `--ci` mode for GitHub Actions
- JUnit XML output (--junit=path)
- TAP output format (--tap)
- Non-zero exit on any failure

## Sample Output
```
=== dsr Test Suite ===
Running 12 test files...

  test_logging.sh          8 tests  PASS  (0.4s)
  test_config.sh          12 tests  PASS  (1.2s)
  test_build_state.sh     15 tests  PASS  (0.8s)
  test_github.sh           6 tests  SKIP  (network)
  test_signing.sh          4 tests  FAIL  (0.3s)
    - test_sign_missing_key: Expected exit 1, got 0

=== Summary ===
Tests:   45 run, 39 passed, 1 failed, 5 skipped
Time:    3.2s

Failed tests:
  scripts/tests/test_signing.sh::test_sign_missing_key
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Discovers and runs all test_*.sh files
- [ ] Produces per-test log files
- [ ] Outputs JSON summary
- [ ] Supports --ci mode
- [ ] Returns non-zero on any failure
- [ ] Supports timeout enforcement
- [ ] Documents all options in --help","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:23.062043997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:36:42.178380240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lm","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:23.062043997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cn","title":"Unit tests for github.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for github.sh Module

## Module Overview
github.sh provides GitHub API access with caching and rate limiting:
- `gh_check` - Verify gh CLI + authentication
- `gh_check_token` - Validate GITHUB_TOKEN
- `gh_api` - Make API requests with caching
- `gh_workflow_runs` - List workflow runs
- `gh_releases` - List releases
- `gh_create_release` - Create a release
- `gh_upload_asset` - Upload release asset

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Authentication Tests
- `gh_check` succeeds when gh is authenticated
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when gh not installed
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when not authenticated
- `gh_check_token` validates GITHUB_TOKEN format
- `gh_check_token` detects expired/invalid tokens (via API call)

### 2. Caching Tests
- Cache directory created under XDG_CACHE_HOME
- First request creates cache entry
- Second request within TTL uses cache
- Request after TTL expiry fetches fresh
- ETag-based validation (304 Not Modified)
- Cache key includes endpoint parameters

### 3. Rate Limiting Tests
- Rate limit detection via response headers
- Retry with backoff on 429 response
- Maximum retry count enforced (3)
- Rate limit warning logged

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Network errors produce clear messages
- 404 responses handled correctly
- 401 (auth) errors trigger re-auth hint
- 500 errors trigger retry logic

### 5. JSON Output Tests
- `--json` mode returns valid JSON
- Error responses follow envelope schema
- Success responses include metadata

## Test Approach
- Test validation/formatting logic WITHOUT live API calls

## Module Overview
host_selector.sh provides host selection and concurrency management:
- `select_host` - Choose optimal host for target platform
- `host_queue_enqueue` - Add build to host queue
- `host_queue_dequeue` - Get next build from queue
- `host_concurrency_check` - Verify host under limit
- `host_capability_match` - Match target to host capabilities

### 1. Host Selection Tests
- Select Linux host for linux/amd64 target
- Select macOS host for darwin/arm64 target
- Select Windows host for windows/amd64 target
- Fallback when preferred host unhealthy
- Error when no capable host available

### 2. Capability Matching Tests
- Host with `capabilities: [rust, go]` matches Go project
- Host without required capability rejected
- Multiple capable hosts returns highest priority

### 3. Concurrency Tests
- Respect per-host concurrency limit
- Queue builds when at capacity
- Dequeue in FIFO order
- Track running builds per host

### 4. Health Integration Tests
- Skip unhealthy hosts in selection
- Re-evaluate after health change
- Log selection rationale

## Test Approach
- Use temp directory for state files
- Mock host health status
- Test deterministic selection (same inputs → same output)
- Verify JSON output format","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:47:46.650337529Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3op","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:51.224832309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- `dsr check --all` checks all registered repos

- `dsr check --json` returns valid JSON
- JSON matches check-details.json schema
- Contains repos_checked, throttled, healthy arrays
- Exit code reflected in envelope

### 3. Exit Codes
- Exit 0 when no repos throttled
- Exit 1 when any repo throttled
- Exit 3 on gh auth failure
- Exit 4 on invalid arguments

### 4. Flag Combinations
- `--quiet` suppresses non-error output
- `--verbose` shows detailed API calls
- `--non-interactive` disables prompts
- `--json --quiet` outputs only JSON

### 5. Edge Cases
- Unknown repo name produces error
- Network failure handled gracefully
- Rate-limited API returns retry hint
- Empty workflow runs list handled

## Test Approach
- Use real dsr binary
- Test against a test repo OR skip network tests
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Capture and verify exit codes
- Use temp config dir for isolation

- Log test name and description
- Log command executed

 {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report

+cmd_version() {
+    local subcmd="${1:-detect}"
+    shift 2>/dev/null || true
+
+    case "$subcmd" in
+        detect)
+            # dsr version detect <tool|path>
+            local target="${1:-}"
+            local repo_path=""
+
+            if [[ -z "$target" ]]; then
+                log_error "Usage: dsr version detect <tool|path>"
+                return 4
+            fi
+
+            # Resolve target to repo path
+            if [[ -d "$target" ]]; then
+                repo_path="$target"
+            else
+                # Try to find tool in repos.d
+                local config_file="${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.d/${target}.yaml"
+                if [[ -f "$config_file" ]] && command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
+                    repo_path=$(yq -r '.local_path // ""' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null)
+                fi
+            fi
+
+            if [[ -z "$repo_path" || ! -d "$repo_path" ]]; then
+                log_error "Cannot resolve target to repository: $target"
+                return 4
+            fi
+
+            if $JSON_MODE; then
+                local json_result
+                json_result=$(version_info_json "$repo_path")
+                json_envelope "version detect" "success" 0 "$json_result"
+            else
+                local version
+                if version=$(version_detect "$repo_path"); then
+                    log_ok "Version: $version (v$version)"
+                    if version_needs_tag "$repo_path"; then
+                        log_info "Tag v$version does not exist (create with: dsr version tag $target)"
+                    else
+                        log_info "Tag v$version already exists"
+                    fi
+                else
+                    log_error "No version file found in $repo_path"
+                    return 1
+                fi
+            fi
+            ;;
+
+        tag)
+            # dsr version tag <tool|path> [--push] [--all]
+            local target="${1:-}"
+            local push=false
+            local tag_all=false
+
+            # Parse options
+            while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+                case "$1" in
+                    --push) push=true; shift ;;
+                    --all) tag_all=true; shift ;;
+                    --dry-run|-n) shift ;;  # Already handled by global DRY_RUN
+                    -*) shift ;;
+                    *)
+                        if [[ -z "$target" || "$target" == --* ]]; then
+                            target="$1"
+                        fi
+                        shift
+                        ;;
+                esac
+            done
+
+            if $tag_all; then
+                # Tag all configured tools
+                local args=""
+                $push && args+=" --push"
+                $DRY_RUN && args+=" --dry-run"
+                $JSON_MODE && args+=" --json"
+                # shellcheck disable=SC2086
+                version_tag_all $args
+                return $?
+            fi
+
+            if [[ -z "$target" ]]; then
+                log_error "Usage: dsr version tag <tool|path> [--push]"
+                log_error "       dsr version tag --all [--push]"
+                return 4
+            fi
+
+            # Resolve target to repo path
+            local repo_path=""
+            if [[ -d "$target" ]]; then
+                repo_path="$target"
+            else
+                local config_file="${DSR_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/dsr}/repos.d/${target}.yaml"
+                if [[ -f "$config_file" ]] && command -v yq &>/dev/null; then
+                    repo_path=$(yq -r '.local_path // ""' "$config_file" 2>/dev/null)

+            fi
+
+            if [[ -z "$repo_path" || ! -d "$repo_path" ]]; then
+                log_error "Cannot resolve target to repository: $target"

+            fi
+
+            local tag_args=""

+

+            if version_create_tag "$repo_path" $tag_args; then

+                fi

+            else

+        --help|-h|help)
+            cat << 'EOF'
+dsr version - Detect and tag versions from version files
+
+USAGE:
+    dsr version <subcommand> [options]
+
+SUBCOMMANDS:
+    detect <tool|path>      Show version from version files
+    tag <tool|path>         Create git tag for detected version
+    tag --all               Tag all configured tools with new versions
+
+OPTIONS:
+    --push          Push tags to remote after creation
+    --dry-run       Show planned actions without executing
+
+VERSION SOURCES:
+    Rust        Cargo.toml       version = "X.Y.Z"
+    Go          VERSION, *.go    Version = "X.Y.Z"
+    Node/Bun    package.json     "version": "X.Y.Z"
+    Python      pyproject.toml   version = "X.Y.Z"
+
+EXAMPLES:
+    dsr version detect ntm          Show version for ntm tool
+    dsr version detect /path/to/repo
+    dsr version tag ntm --push      Create and push tag
+    dsr version tag --all           Tag all tools needing tags
+    dsr version tag --all --push    Tag and push all
+
+For more information, see docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md
+EOF
+            ;;
+
+        *)
+            log_error "Unknown version subcommand: $subcmd"
+            log_info "Run 'dsr version --help' for usage"
+            return 4
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
